action.reasoning: Observation: the 3-model ensemble with weights 0.30/0.20/0.50 is a large new best (balanced_accuracy 0.6579), showing the seed-123 model adds strong complementary bobcat/cat/empty/raccoon performance but costs some bird/dog/opossum/squirrel recall versus the previous A/B ensemble. Analysis: a cheap weight sweep around this new optimum is likely worthwhile. Reducing the seed-123 weight slightly may recover bird/squirrel/dog/opossum while retaining most of its gains on bobcat/cat/empty. Decision: evaluate a nearby 3-model ensemble with 45% seed-123 and 55% previous 0.6/0.4 A/B mixture, i.e. weights 0.33/0.22/0.45.
action.command:
python - <<'PY'
import torch
from pathlib import Path
out=Path('checkpoints/dino_ens_4_10_s123_w332245.pt')
ckpt={
  'type':'ensemble',
  'members':['checkpoints/dino_4e.pt','checkpoints/dino_10e.pt','checkpoints/dino_4e_s123.pt'],
  'weights':[0.33,0.22,0.45],
}
torch.save(ckpt,out)
print(f'saved {out}')
PY
